summary: Decompiled five small miscellaneous functions across game-mode register clear, stage Castle, stage Old Pupupu, and trophy/toy display code. The PR replaced UNK stubs for fn_8017DE54, gm_80180BA0, fn_801CFAFC, grOldPupupu_80211C1C, and un_8031BB34 with matching C, updated affected prototypes and forward includes, and refined one gmregclear static data struct. The submitted test evidence says ninja builds, ninja diff has no regressions, and all touched functions are 100% objdiff matches.
what_changed: Implemented fn_8017DE54 as an s32-returning helper taking a u8 flag byte and u8 pointer, with branch behavior for bits 0x20, 0x10, and 0x02 and sentinel checks against value 0x21.
Implemented gm_80180BA0 as a void function that loops over 0x1B entries, maps each loop index through gm_80164024, and writes lbl_80472E48.x14[idx] * 0xA through gmMainLib_8015D06C.
Refined struct lbl_80472E48_t by replacing a large pad_C[0x74] blob with pad_C[0x8] plus s32 x14[0x1B], while preserving the 0x80 size static assertion.

Implemented fn_801CFAFC in grcastle with typed parameters Item_GObj*, Ground*, Vec3*, HSD_GObj*, and f32; it writes castle state value 4 into gp->gv.castle2.xC4 and conditionally calls ftLib_80086A4C when ftLib_80086960 succeeds.
Updated grcastle.h to include melee/it/forward.h so the Item_GObj prototype can be typed instead of UNK_PARAMS.

Added static int grOp_804D48E0[1][2] = {{0, 0}} in groldpupupu and implemented grOldPupupu_80211C1C to fetch another ground object via Ground_801C2BA4(7), inspect gv.unk fields, and call grAnime_801C8138 with a table-derived animation argument.
Implemented un_8031BB34 in tydisplay as an s32-returning lookup into a local string table of ToyDsp*_Top_joint names, normalizing idx -1 to 0.
Updated gmregclear.h and grcastle.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to typed signatures for the newly decompiled functions.
smart_moves: Converted unknown prototypes at the same time as the C implementations, which makes the decomp useful to future callers and reduces UNK_PARAMS spread.
Kept lbl_80472E48_t size protected with STATIC_ASSERT while exposing the x14 array needed by gm_80180BA0, avoiding a risky total struct rewrite.
Used a narrow forward include, melee/it/forward.h, to support Item_GObj typing in grcastle.h without pulling in heavier item headers.
Matched gm_80180BA0 using an explicit u8 cast for the loop index and a local u8 idx, reflecting the likely ABI/codegen constraints for gm_80164024 calls.
Preserved the odd-looking double call to gm_80164024 in gm_80180BA0 rather than over-simplifying to only use idx, likely because that shape was needed for matching.
For grOldPupupu_80211C1C, included PAD_STACK(8), showing the match required explicit stack padding rather than cleaner source-only style.
Reconstructed un_8031BB34 as a direct local table of exact asset/joint strings, including the repeated ToyDspQues_Top_joint entry at the end, instead of trying to deduplicate or infer a formula.
decomp_lessons: When a global struct is mostly unknown, expose only the field range required by the matched function and keep padding plus STATIC_ASSERT to avoid overclaiming layout knowledge.
Small callback-like stage functions can often be typed by examining adjacent functions and call sites; here grcastle.h could move from UNK_PARAMS to Item_GObj*, Ground*, Vec3*, HSD_GObj*, f32.
Do not remove redundant-looking calls if they are part of the observed matching shape; gm_80180BA0 stores the result of gm_80164024 but also calls it again for gmMainLib_8015D06C.
Sentinel-heavy byte logic may need explicit casts to s32 for comparisons to produce matching codegen, as shown in fn_8017DE54's checks against 0x21.
Asset-name lookup helpers may return integer-cast pointers in legacy signatures; un_8031BB34 returns s32 even though the table entries are char*.
Stage animation helpers may depend on tiny static tables even when all visible values are zero; adding the data symbol can be necessary for a function match.
naming_conventions: Existing address-based names were retained for unknown functions: fn_8017DE54, gm_80180BA0, fn_801CFAFC, grOldPupupu_80211C1C, and un_8031BB34.
Stage-specific public names keep their subsystem prefix and address suffix, such as grOldPupupu_80211C1C and grCastle_801CF7B0.
Unidentified global/static data follows the module prefix plus address convention, such as lbl_80472E48, grOp_804D6A9C, and grOp_804D48E0.
Unknown struct fields remain placeholder-style with offsets, such as x14, xC4, xC8, xCC, xD4, and xD8, rather than receiving speculative semantic names.
Trophy display strings preserve exact asset capitalization, e.g. ToyDspMycharaCmA_Top_joint, ToyDspFgseriesA_Top_joint, and ToyDspPokemonA_Top_joint.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(8) in grOldPupupu_80211C1C to satisfy stack layout requirements.
Used explicit u8 casts around loop indices in gm_80180BA0 to steer integer width and calling convention behavior.
Used explicit s32 casts for byte comparisons in fn_8017DE54, likely to align compare instruction selection.
Cast &gp->gv.castle2.xC4 to s16* before assigning 4 in fn_801CFAFC, indicating the known field type/layout did not directly match the store width needed for matching.
Represented the Old Pupupu animation selector as a 2D static int table grOp_804D48E0[1][2], matching the indexed access form grOp_804D48E0[xCC][xD8].
Returned (s32) table[idx] from un_8031BB34, preserving the observed integer return type despite pointer data.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body reports successful validation with ninja, ninja diff, and 100% objdiff matches for all functions.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or further type lbl_80472E48_t.x14 in gmregclear.
Check whether fn_8017DE54 receives a semantic name after its flag and sentinel behavior is understood.
Search for callers of fn_801CFAFC to confirm the Item_GObj, Ground, Vec3, HSD_GObj, f32 callback signature.
Look for later Old Pupupu work that expands grOp_804D48E0 beyond the placeholder {{0, 0}} table.
Compare un_8031BB34 with un_8031BB94 and neighboring tydisplay lookup functions for possible shared trophy display table semantics.
